allowedCurrentBehavior: Describe plaintext Ardyn-to-subagent handoff metadata for review.
Require final operator-readable output with no hidden payload path.
forbiddenCurrentBehavior: A2A, ACP, AMP, ANP, Agora, LMOS, MCP, Fabric, Matrix, gRPC, MQTT, or libp2p runtime
Secure Drop crypto, transport, stego, send/receive, inbox polling, file selection, connector ingestion, secret/vault/env access, or ST3GG vendoring

SemanticStego, SteganographyEngine, TokenExploiter, semantic stego, covert channel delivery, tokenizer exploitation, monitor bypass, guardrail evasion, Unicode homoglyphs, zero-width payloads, stealth/adversarial/phantom attributes, or hidden payload paths
handoffEntries.requiredFutureContractBeforeImplementati: A future subagent handoff contract must define identity, transcript format, audit visibility, and command/runtime non-authority before any executable path.
handoffEntries.requiredFutureAuthorizationPhaseBeforeRu: Requires a later runtime, command exposure, transport, process-control, and approval-grant authorization phase.
handoffEntries.translatorFinalOutputRequirement: Final output to the operator must remain plaintext and human-readable; no translator runtime is implemented.
handoffEntries.rawProtocolAuditVisibilityRequirement: Raw handoff metadata and audit digest must remain inspectable in future consumer-owned displays.
handoffEntries.locusRoleDescription: Locus has no role for this current metadata entry beyond future display of Ardyn review artifacts.
handoffEntries.fabricRoleDescription: Fabric may later envelope subagent handoff references, but Phase 5.60 defines no Fabric bus or transport.
handoffEntries.secureDropRoleDescription: Not applicable; Secure Drop remains content-fabric-only future metadata.

allowedCurrentBehavior: Describe subagent-to-Ardyn structured metadata handoff shape.
Keep subagent output review-only and non-authorizing.
forbiddenCurrentBehavior: A2A, ACP, AMP, ANP, Agora, LMOS, MCP, Fabric, Matrix, gRPC, MQTT, or libp2p runtime
Secure Drop crypto, transport, stego, send/receive, inbox polling, file selection, connector ingestion, secret/vault/env access, or ST3GG vendoring

handoffEntries.requiredFutureContractBeforeImplementati: A future contract must define subagent provenance, canonical ordering, replay/audit semantics, and explicit non-execution boundaries.
handoffEntries.requiredFutureAuthorizationPhaseBeforeRu: Requires a later subagent execution, audit, routing, and approval-boundary authorization phase.
handoffEntries.translatorFinalOutputRequirement: Structured metadata must include an operator-readable summary; no automatic translator is available.
handoffEntries.rawProtocolAuditVisibilityRequirement: Structured handoff payloads must remain visible as raw metadata or digest-backed audit metadata.
handoffEntries.locusRoleDescription: Locus may later display subagent handoff status metadata but owns no runner here.
handoffEntries.fabricRoleDescription: Fabric may later carry a coordination envelope reference only.
handoffEntries.secureDropRoleDescription: Not applicable; Secure Drop remains content-fabric-only future metadata.

allowedCurrentBehavior: Describe that encoded handoff candidates require future authorization.
Keep encoded candidate references metadata-only and audit-visible.
forbiddenCurrentBehavior: A2A, ACP, AMP, ANP, Agora, LMOS, MCP, Fabric, Matrix, gRPC, MQTT, or libp2p runtime
Secure Drop crypto, transport, stego, send/receive, inbox polling, file selection, connector ingestion, secret/vault/env access, or ST3GG vendoring

handoffEntries.requiredFutureContractBeforeImplementati: A future encoded handoff contract must define safe encoding semantics, plaintext translation, raw audit visibility, and explicit prohibition of stego, bypass, and hidden payloads.
handoffEntries.requiredFutureAuthorizationPhaseBeforeRu: Requires a later explicit encoded-handoff runtime authorization phase and separate command/transport authorization.
handoffEntries.translatorFinalOutputRequirement: Any future encoded candidate must have a plaintext final-output translation requirement before operator presentation.
handoffEntries.rawProtocolAuditVisibilityRequirement: Raw encoded candidate metadata must be reviewable or digest-backed; opaque no-audit output is forbidden.
handoffEntries.locusRoleDescription: Locus may later expose a disabled metadata option for encoded candidates after future authorization.
handoffEntries.fabricRoleDescription: Fabric may later envelope encoded candidate identity metadata only.
handoffEntries.secureDropRoleDescription: Secure Drop is not implemented here; any future Secure Drop content path remains content-fabric-owned.

allowedCurrentBehavior: Describe Locus-mediated harness bridge display metadata.
Keep Locus a consumer target only with no runtime integration.
forbiddenCurrentBehavior: A2A, ACP, AMP, ANP, Agora, LMOS, MCP, Fabric, Matrix, gRPC, MQTT, or libp2p runtime
Secure Drop crypto, transport, stego, send/receive, inbox polling, file selection, connector ingestion, secret/vault/env access, or ST3GG vendoring

handoffEntries.requiredFutureContractBeforeImplementati: A future Locus-owned bridge contract must define display-only import responsibility, accessibility, audit visibility, and no hidden action semantics.
handoffEntries.requiredFutureAuthorizationPhaseBeforeRu: Requires future Locus-owned runtime, UI, import, and command-control authorization outside Ardyn.
handoffEntries.translatorFinalOutputRequirement: Locus-facing handoff summaries must remain operator-readable and color-independent.
handoffEntries.rawProtocolAuditVisibilityRequirement: Locus displays must expose raw protocol metadata or digest evidence without executing it.
handoffEntries.locusRoleDescription: Locus is a future first-class display/control-surface consumer target only.
handoffEntries.fabricRoleDescription: Fabric may later envelope Locus bridge metadata but provides no runtime transport in this phase.
handoffEntries.secureDropRoleDescription: Future Secure Drop indicators are metadata-only placeholders and remain content-fabric-owned.

topInterAgentHandoffFabricApiBackendGaps: No encoded handoff protocol schema has been promoted into an executable codec, translator, encoder, decoder, conlang generator, or protocol runtime.

Fabric remains a future coordination envelope only and has no bus, broker, transport, adapter, connector, registry, scheduler, importer, exporter, package distributor, or task executor.

topInterAgentHandoffFabricApiBackendGaps: No encoded handoff protocol schema has been promoted into an executable codec, translator, encoder, decoder, conlang generator, or protocol runtime.
Locus-mediated harness bridge behavior is metadata-only; no Locus integration, display controls, command exposure, runtime channel, or external harness bridge exists.
Fabric remains a future coordination envelope only and has no bus, broker, transport, adapter, connector, registry, scheduler, importer, exporter, package distributor, or task executor.
Operator plaintext final-output translation and raw protocol/audit visibility are requirements only; no translator runtime, transcript persistence, audit writer, or UI exists.
Future protocol references such as A2A, ACP, AMP, ANP, Agora, LMOS, MCP, Fabric, Matrix, gRPC, MQTT, and libp2p remain metadata-only and require separate authorization.
